user.title: RTL-433 Event Topic
user.description: Configure the MQTT topic where rtl_433 publishes device events. This integration uses HA's built-in MQTT integration for connectivity.

confirm_device.title: Add RTL-433 Device: {model}

abort.already_in_progress: A setup flow for this device is already in progress.

data.next_step: What would you like to do?

init_device.title: Device Options: {current_name}
init_device.description:
Manage device **{device_id}**.

Rename the device or reset it back to discovered state (this removes its entities from HA).

manage_devices.title: Manage Discovered Devices
manage_devices.description: Found {device_count} devices ({approved_count} approved, {ignored_count} ignored). Select devices to approve for Home Assistant or ignore to hide them. Approved devices can be given a friendly alias.

merge_devices.title: Merge Suggestions
merge_devices.description: Found {merge_count} potential device merges. These are new devices that match the model and channel of an existing approved device that has gone stale — likely a rolling ID change after battery replacement. Select merges to accept.

settings.title: Discovery Settings
settings.description: Fine-tune how rtl_433 devices are discovered and published.

data.force_update: Force Update (publish even if value unchanged)

abort.no_devices_discovered: No devices have been discovered yet. Wait for rtl_433 to receive some signals.

approve_device.name: Approve Device
approve_device.description: Approve an rtl_433 device for Home Assistant entity creation.

alias.name: Alias
alias.description: Friendly name for the device (e.g. Weather Station)

ignore_device.name: Ignore Device
ignore_device.description: Ignore an rtl_433 device (blocklist it from appearing).

merge_device.name: Merge Device
merge_device.description: Merge a new device into an existing approved device (rolling ID change).

old_device_id.name: Old Device ID
old_device_id.description: The existing approved device to merge into

reset_device.name: Reset Device
reset_device.description: Reset a device back to 'discovered' state.
